programme.title: Forbidden Science - Digital Desire
programme.desc: Forbidden Science is a unique blend of noir science fiction and erotic programming. Set in the near future, the stories revolve around the evil corporation, 4Ever Innovations, whose young and brilliant scientists download and sell memories. They replace those we've lost with clones and devise technology that helps us live out our greatest fantasies; from saving a failed relationship, to creating the perfect lover, to being irresistible to the opposite sex - but sometimes our deepest desires come at a terrible price. 4Ever offers customers the opportunity to live out their fantasies in a virtual world but there are secrets within 4Ever Innovations that may cost you your life.

programme.title: Phoenix Lights: UFO Flyovers or Military Test Flights?

programme.title: Inca Civilization: Defeated or Diseased?

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: Surviving Mann Season 1 thrilled with action, adventure, and good old-fashioned American determination. But, before Season 2 arrives, Don has a new challenge for the best of the best. In All-Stars the top 3 Surviving Mann contestants face off against 3 elite special ops veterans in a 50-hour no-sleep marathon battle of endurance, skill, and willpower. Do the All-Stars have what it takes to take on these spec ops legends? Find in Surviving Mann: All-Stars.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: For the first time in Surviving Mann history, the contestants undergo intense combat training led by none other than Ken Shamrock—"the most dangerous man in the world." His relentless situation drills push this elite group beyond their limits, sharpening their instincts and forging next-level tactical readiness. As a new mission begins to take shape, the pressure mounts—and the team learns that the strongest among them will earn a game-changing reward. But in a world where every edge matters, not everyone is built to keep up. Fatigue sets in, tensions rise, and the difference between teammate and competitor begins to blur. With Shamrock watching closely, only the most disciplined will rise to meet the challenge—and prove they’re worthy of what lies ahead.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Climbing to the top, literally! In this episode, you better put your fear of heights away. The contestants have the option to earn extra points, but not everyone is happy about it. Mental strength is tested through rappelling from high above the sky. Who will come out on top?

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Planks, Don! Don gave the contestants an option to earn extra points for planking. How does this shake up the top points earners? And of course, there’s shooting challenges. First, Don puts the contestants through realistic military training for a car ambush scenario. Then, three-time state champion, Renee Enchautegui, takes the 32 on a night-time shooting challenge.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: As the mission gets closer, the 32 must prepare for various scenarios, including CQB - Close Quarters Battle - which can be essential in hostage rescue. Watch the live-fire CQB drill teams and see how they stack up. Plus Don & Randy give the top competitors one last chance to rack up BIG points with another 700 yard Roll-The-Dice Pinnacle Shot. Find out who won... and who lost, in this episode of Surviving Mann.

programme.title: Through X
programme.desc: Joey Spallina - Paul Carcaterra visits Syracuse, N.Y. to pull back the curtain on the biggest name in College Lacrosse: Joey Spallina. In this episode of THROUGH X, Spallina speaks on wearing the No. 22 at Syracuse, shares his goal to return the Orange to NCAA Championship glory, and shows you how to score his signature behind-the-back goal.

programme.title: Through X
programme.desc: Paul Carcaterra heads to Evanston, IL to visit with Northwestern's Izzy Scane. Izzy has been a dominant force for the Wildcats since she first arrived on campus. With a National title, the Tewaaraton Award winner, and record shattering season – she has cemented herself as an all-time great. We also went to Charlottesville, VA to dissect the elite game of Connor Shellenberger: a hometown hero, and arguably the best player in history for the University of Virginia.

programme.title: Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show
programme.desc: Welcome to Episode 13 of the Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show presented by Duke Cannon. In this episode, we break down everything that happened in the NCAA Quarterfinals and look ahead to the Championship Weekend matchups. The crew also welcomes Matt Nestler on the show to discuss the news he broke about Lars Tiffany being out as head coach at Virginia, then we chop it up with the boys from The D3 Pod.

programme.title: Connor Shellenberger | 2025 Season Highlights
programme.desc: The New York Atlas attackman had a historic sophomore season including the Jim Brown MVP Award and a Championship.

programme.title: Marcus Holman and the Moment He Became Obsessed with Lacrosse
programme.desc: Kyle Harrison sits down with Marcus Holman - one of lacrosse’s greatest shooters and leaders - for an honest conversation about family, obsession, adversity, longevity, and chasing the all-time goals record. Marcus takes us back to his earliest memories from John's Hopkins’ locker room to the moment he fell in love with shooting He opens up about evolving his role, becoming a leader, sustaining greatness into his mid-30s, and what it would mean to cement his legacy in the record books.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.

programme.title: Texas: Route 66
programme.desc: To celebrate the 100th anniversary of America's first major highway, Jefferson Graham takes Photowalks to the Route 66 portion of Texas, a relatively tiny portion of the major road.

programme.title: Darwin's War
programme.desc: An inside look into the Ukrainian FPV Kamikaze drone unit hunting Russian soldiers on the frontlines.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.

programme.title: Darwin's War
programme.desc: An inside look into the Ukrainian FPV Kamikaze drone unit hunting Russian soldiers on the frontlines.

programme.title: Rumbling Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau examines the cause of volcanoes, earthquakes and tsunamis. We visit the Big Island of Hawaii, where there is still an active volcano. Then we visit a laboratory at Oregon State University that is able to simulate a tsunami. We'll explore the damage that these massive waves cause. Then we head to the Puget Sound in Washington and discover the history of tsunamis in the Northwest with the help of geologist, Brian Atwater. We'll learn if a tsunami will happen again on the west coast.
